decision_taken: NOT implemented — the spine store models the FLAT vocabulary (stock term fields name/description/weight + latest-wins + Drupal default ordering). Drupal core taxonomy terms carry a `parent` field (TermInterface::parent) supporting hierarchy, ancestry, and cycle-prevention, but NONE of the four leaf modules (farm_product_type/season/test_method/unit) introduce or configure it — their install config is vid+name+description+weight only. Per the wave plan, core/* is ported once as a kernel+adapter; term hierarchy belongs to that core-taxonomy adapter, not these leaf shells.
evidence: modules/taxonomy/{product_type,season,test_method,unit}/config/install/taxonomy.vocabulary.*.yml declare only {langcode,status,dependencies,name,vid,description,weight,new_revision}; no hierarchy/parent config. partition-2026-07-22.jsonl: vocab_new 0, 'no new fields/values/measures'. This is a non-trivial fold (transitive ancestry + cycle guard) and is a DECLARED GAP, not hand-rolled.
reversal_condition: the core-taxonomy adapter lands term hierarchy and a spine-b feature is shown to depend on parent/children, OR a fixture family exercises parent-scoped term listing against one of these vocabularies

decision_taken: recorded as a verbatim VocabularySpec field for fidelity but INERT in the spine store — the vocabularies set new_revision:false, and term revision history is a core-taxonomy entity concern out of scope for a vocabulary shell.
evidence: each taxonomy.vocabulary.*.yml carries new_revision:false; no revision fixtures implied by vocab_new 0.
reversal_condition: a vocabulary in this family flips new_revision:true and a fixture reads prior term revisions
